scenarios.rationale: Windows Server 2012 is EOL (Oct 2023). Must upgrade to Windows Server 2022/2025 to restore security patch coverage.
.NET Core is cross-platform. Migrating to Linux OS would reduce Windows Server licensing costs and leverage Linux ecosystem.
.NET 6+ supports ARM64 natively. If .NET version is confirmed current, AWS Graviton migration is feasible.
IIS 8.0 is EOL (Oct 2023). Upgrade to IIS 10 on Windows Server 2022, or migrate to Kestrel/Nginx if moving to Linux.
Partial cloud deployment on AWS exists but hybrid on-premise footprint remains. Full cloud migration pending.
2-Tier architecture could benefit from refactoring to microservices, especially to separate HR modules (payroll, benefits, employee records).
SQL Server 2019 is in extended support. Upgrade to SQL Server 2022 is recommended to extend support to 2033.
On-premise SQL Server could move to Amazon RDS SQL Server or Azure SQL to eliminate DBA management overhead.
SQL Server on ARM is not available as a managed service from AWS or Azure. Not applicable.
HR/payroll database has consistent load patterns and strict ACID requirements. Serverless DB is not suitable.
Migrating from SQL Server 2019 to PostgreSQL would eliminate SQL Server licensing. High effort but significant long-term savings.
